questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $\sqrt{1-x^6}+\sqrt{1-y^6}=a\left(x^3-y^3\right)$ and $\frac{d y}{d x}=f(x, y) \sqrt{\frac{1-y^6}{1-x^6}}$ then,
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & -\frac{x^5}{\sqrt{1-x^6}}-\frac{y^5}{\sqrt{1-y^6}} \frac{d y}{d x}=a\left(x^2-y^2 \frac{d y}{d x}\right) \\ & \Rightarrow\left(a y^2-\frac{y^5}{\sqrt{1-y^6}}\right) \frac{d y}{d x}=a x^2+\frac{x^5}{\sqrt{1-x^6}}\end{aligned}$
$\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{\sqrt{1-y^6}}{\sqrt{1-x^6}} \times \frac{x^2\left(a \sqrt{1-x^6}+x^3\right)}{y^2\left(a \sqrt{1-y^6}-y^3\right)} \quad$....(i)
Also, $\frac{\left(1-x^6\right)-\left(1-y^6\right)}{\sqrt{1-x^6}-\sqrt{1-y^6}}=a\left(x^3-y^3\right)$ or $x^3+y^3=a\left(\sqrt{1-y^6}-\sqrt{1-x^6}\right)$ $\Rightarrow a \sqrt{1-y^6}-y^3=a \sqrt{1-x^6}+x^3$
Hence, from equation (1), $\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{\sqrt{1-y^6}}{\sqrt{1-x^6}} \times \frac{x^2}{y^2}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Let $y=f(x)$ be a curve passing through $\left(e, e^e\right)$, which satisfy the differential equation
$(2 n y+x y \ln x) d x-x \ln x d y=0, x\gt0, y\gt0$. It $g(x)=\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} f(x)$ then $\int_{1 / e}^e g(x) d x$ equals to
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & f(x)=e^x(\ln x)^{2 n} \\ & \lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} f(x)=0, \text { if } x \in\left(\frac{1}{e}, e\right) \\ & \therefore \int_{1 / e}^e g(x) d x=0\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let I be the purchase value of an equipment and $V(t)$ be the value after it has been used for $t$ years. The value $\mathrm{V}(\mathrm{t})$ depreciates at a rate given by differential equation $\frac{\mathrm{dV}(\mathrm{t})}{\mathrm{dt}}=-\mathrm{K}(\mathrm{T}-\mathrm{t})$, where $\mathrm{K}\gt0$ is a constant and T is the total life in years of the equipment. Then the scrap value $V(T)$ of the equipment is
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& \frac{d V(t)}{d t}=-K(T-t) \\
& \Rightarrow V(t)=\frac{K(T-t)^2}{2}+C
\end{aligned}$
At $\mathrm{t}=\mathrm{O}, \mathrm{V}=\mathrm{I} \Rightarrow \mathrm{C}=\mathrm{I}-\frac{\mathrm{KT}^2}{2}$
$\therefore$ Scrap value $\mathrm{V}(\mathrm{T}) \Rightarrow \mathrm{V}(\mathrm{t}=\mathrm{T})=\mathrm{C}=\mathrm{I}-\frac{\mathrm{KT}^2}{2}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Solution of the differential equation $\left(x \frac{d y}{d x}+y\right)=e^{x y-\ln x^2}\left(x \frac{d y}{d x}-y\right)$
questions.solution:
Clearly we have
$\begin{aligned}
& \frac{x d y+y d x}{e^{x y}}=\frac{x d y-y d x}{x^2} \\
& \Rightarrow \int d(x y) e^{-x y}=\int d\left(\frac{y}{x}\right) \Rightarrow \frac{y}{x}+e^{-x y}=c
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If the given curve satisfies the differential equation $e^y d x+\left(x e^y+2 y\right) d y=0$ and also passes through $(0,0)$ then the possible equation of curve can be
questions.solution:
$M=e^y, N=x \cdot e^y+2 y$
Now, $\frac{\mathrm{dM}}{\mathrm{dy}}$ (keeping x constant $)=\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{y}}$
$=\frac{\mathrm{dN}}{\mathrm{dx}}$ (keeping y constant)
Hence, the given equation is exact
Now, $f=\int e^y d x+g(y)=x \cdot e^y+g(y)$
Differentiating w.r.t $y$ we get, $x \cdot e^y+\frac{d g}{d y}=N=x \cdot e^y+2 y$
i.e. $\frac{d g}{d y}=2 y$
Integrating $g(y)=y^2+c_1$
$\Rightarrow \mathrm{f}=\mathrm{x} \cdot \mathrm{e}^y+\mathrm{y}^2+\mathrm{c}_1$
So, desired solution is $x \cdot \mathrm{e}^y+\mathrm{y}^2=0$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
